objective: Ship a prerendered `/portfolio` route that showcases design work: (1) a hero section above Featured Works highlighting one portfolio project, defaulting to the most recent by circa date but overridable via content data; (2) a responsive 3-column grid (1 column on small viewports) styled like the reference (dark theme, glass header area, neon-green filter links, glossy diagonal overlay on thumbnails); (3) a TypeScript-only light CMS under `$lib/content/` (one file per content domain per ADR-008) holding entries with: name, project type (Branding & Identity | Illustration | User Interface), three image URLs (thumbnail, hero, full), circa date (string), technologies[], one-line summary, full description, optional live project URL; (4) client-free filtering: show only projects matching the selected type; filters exposed as normal navigable links; optional query-based routing using `history.replaceState` (no extra history entries) when JS runs, with server-rendered HTML correct for no-JS; (5) grid items and hero link toward a project detail route that is explicitly out of scope (use `#` or a stub path only if the plan requires a target); (6) use placeholder imagery (e.g. placehold.co) until real assets exist. Project detail page implementation is out of scope.

completed_stages.summary: Plan and acceptance criteria written for portfolio route, design-portfolio content domain, prerender entries, assembler, and PE filters.
Portfolio route, content domain, assembler, prerender config, stub detail route; check and lint passed; build noted EBUSY on Windows.
Vitest coverage for portfolio load helpers, content, assembler, route load; test-report maps AC-01-AC-22.
Verdict FAIL: AC-12/AC-13 vs SvelteKit prerender URL API and Windows prerender filenames.
Remediation: document href-based query parse; POSIX vs win32 prerender entries; PageData narrowing; parity test for type query.
Verdict PASS_WITH_NOTES: AC and ADRs satisfied with documented POSIX CI note for query prerender.
